vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1832: Role Models — support multiple models per role with weighted distribution (load-spreading across providers)

plan.title: Role Models: support multiple models per role with weighted distribution (load-spreading across providers)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Each pipeline role binds to a single model. When a fleet runs many agents of one role they all hit the same provider and exhaust its rate limits (gpt-5.5 reviewer stalls, #1830). We hand-split the fleet ~30/70 gpt-5.5/kimi to spread load. This makes the split first-class: a role's model may be a list of {model, weight} entries, sampled per-spawn.
narratives.Proposal: Make RoleConfig.model polymorphic (ModelRef | WeightedModelRef[]). Add a stateless, deterministic weighted sampler (32-bit FNV-1a over a per-spawn key `${role}:${issueId}`; weights relative/normalized). resolveModel samples only when the role-level model is an array and a spawnKey is supplied; display/router paths get the highest-weight representative. Sample once at the role-level spawn site, thread the concrete model — harness then follows from the sampled model via resolveHarness (provider-default-only, PAN-1984), so one role can mix harnesses with no harness-layer change. Extend both validation surfaces (config-load + settings API) and the Settings → Roles picker to the list form, top-level roles only.
narratives.NonGoals:
- No exact/stride distribution (no persistent per-role spawn counter / new state store).
- No distributions on sub-roles (work.inspect, review.security, …) or on workhorse slots — they keep the scalar ModelRef type.
- No per-model maxAgents governor accounting (issue open question — deferred to a follow-up).
- No new aggregated 'realized split' dashboard view — the per-agent model is already recorded in state.json and shown per agent.
- No change to resolveHarness precedence or the ToS gate (canUseHarnessSync).
- No change to --resume/JSONL behavior beyond honoring the already-persisted model (no resample on restart).

items.title: Add WeightedModelRef/RoleModelRef types and the deterministic weighted sampler

narrative.Action: In src/lib/config-yaml.ts add `interface WeightedModelRef { model: ModelRef; weight: number }` and `type RoleModelRef = ModelRef | WeightedModelRef[]`, and change `RoleConfig.model` to `RoleModelRef`. Add pure helpers: `fnv1a32(s: string): number` (32-bit FNV-1a, no Math.random/Date.now), `pickWeightedModelRef(entries, spawnKey): ModelRef` (relative weights: hash mod sum-of-positive-weights → cumulative band; throws if no positive weight), and `representativeModelRef(entries): ModelRef` (highest weight, first on tie). No call-site behavior change yet — this bead only adds the types + helpers and their unit tests. Defaults (DEFAULT_MODEL_REFS, DEFAULT_ROLES) stay scalar. RoleSubConfig.model stays ModelRef (scalar).

items.title: pickWeightedModelRef returns the SAME model for a given spawnKey on repeated calls (deterministic), and the type change compiles.
Over 1000 distinct keys, pickWeightedModelRef on [{a,70},{b,30}] produces counts within ±5 percentage points of 70/30 (realizes the intended ratio).
pickWeightedModelRef returns identical per-key picks for [{a,7},{b,3}] and [{a,70},{b,30}] — weights are proportional, not sum=100.
representativeModelRef returns the highest-weight entry's model (first entry on a weight tie).
pickWeightedModelRef throws when given a list whose entries all have weight <= 0 (no silent default).
Extend resolveModel to sample an array role model by spawnKey

narrative.Action: In src/lib/config-yaml.ts add an optional `spawnKey?: string` parameter to `resolveModel(role, subRole?, config, spawnKey?)`. After computing `ref = subModel ?? roleModel ?? DEFAULT_MODEL_REFS[role]`: if `ref` is an array, choose `pickWeightedModelRef(ref, spawnKey)` when spawnKey is provided else `representativeModelRef(ref)`, then `derefWorkhorse(picked, config, fieldPath)`. Scalar refs are unchanged. Because subModel (a sub-role's scalar model) takes precedence, a sub-role resolve never reaches the array branch. derefWorkhorse still resolves workhorse refs inside picked entries.

items.title: resolveModel('work', undefined, config, key) with an array role model returns the derefed model id of the entry sampled by that key.
resolveModel('work', undefined, config) WITHOUT a spawnKey, with an array role model, returns the representative (highest-weight) model — deterministic for display/router paths.
A scalar role model resolves to exactly the same model id as before, with or without a spawnKey (back-compat preserved).
resolveModel('work', 'inspect', config, key) returns the sub-role's scalar model and never samples (sub-role precedence holds even when the parent role model is an array).
resolveModel resolves a workhorse ref (e.g. workhorse:mid) used as a distribution entry to its concrete model id after sampling.
Validate the distribution form at config load and preserve it across merge

narrative.Action: In src/lib/config-yaml.ts validateRoleFields(role, roleConfig) (load path ~:1711): when roleConfig.model is an array, require a non-empty list where every entry is { model: non-empty string, weight: positive integer }; throw a precise `config.yaml: roles.<role>.model[<i>].weight …` error otherwise. Arrays are valid ONLY for the top-level role model — reject array-shaped sub-role/workhorse models. Confirm mergeRoleConfig (~:1658) and cloneRoles (~:1478) replace the `model` key wholesale (spread already does this); defensively clone the array in cloneRoles so a later mutation cannot alias the merged config.

items.title: loadConfig accepts a valid roles.work.model distribution and resolveModel returns the array's sampled model unchanged.
An empty distribution array for a role model throws a field-path config-load error.
config load rejects a distribution entry whose weight is 0, negative, or non-integer, with a precise field-path error.
An existing scalar role-model config still loads and resolves unchanged.
Merging a layer that sets a distribution over a base scalar model produces the array as the model (replaced wholesale, not a merged object).
Extend settings-api validateModelRef to the distribution form (roles only)

narrative.Action: In src/lib/settings-api.ts add an `allowDistribution = false` parameter to validateModelRef (~:417). When ref is an array AND allowDistribution: require non-empty, each entry weight a positive integer, and recurse the existing scalar checks on each entry.model (reusing allowWorkhorseRef). When ref is an array and NOT allowDistribution: push a 'distribution not allowed here' error. Pass allowDistribution=true ONLY at the roles.<role>.model call (~:531); workhorses (~:511) and sub-roles (~:563) keep false. Extend the model-aware effort check (~:534-547): when the model is a distribution, the role effort must be supported by EVERY entry's resolved model (resolveModelRefToId per entry); reject otherwise.

items.title: PUT /api/settings with a valid roles.work.model distribution returns success and persists the array to config.
The settings API rejects a distribution with a non-positive/non-integer weight, an invalid model ref, or an empty list, with a descriptive validation error.
The settings API rejects a distribution placed on a sub-role model or a workhorse slot (allowDistribution is false there).
The settings API rejects a role-level effort that is not supported by at least one model in the distribution.
A scalar model ref still validates exactly as before (no regression).
Thread a per-spawn sampling key into the role-level spawn paths

narrative.Action: Add an optional `spawnKey?: string` to determineModel({model, role, spawnKey}) in src/lib/agents.ts (~:2791) and pass it into resolveModel(role, undefined, config, spawnKey). Thread `${role}:${issueId}` from the role-level spawn sites: determineModel callers at routes/agents.ts:2981 (dashboard start), agents.ts:3308 (spawnRun), sequencer-agent.ts:53; plus the direct role-level resolveModel calls at spawn-planning-session.ts:553 (plan), review-agent.ts:306 (only when opts.subRole is undefined — the review orchestrator), and specialist-context.ts:128 (when subRole undefined). Do NOT add a key to display/router callers (router-config.ts, misc.ts, specialists.ts, flywheel.ts, flywheel-actions.ts) — they intentionally get the representative. Preserve the sample-once invariant: determineModel returns a non-empty `model` override unchanged, so downstream re-entry (runtimes/claude-code.ts:350) and restart/resume (agents.ts:3637) must pass the already-persisted concrete model, never re-resolve from scratch.

items.title: determineModel({role:'work', spawnKey}) with a work distribution returns the same model for the same key and can return different models for different keys.
Each enumerated role-level spawn site passes a `${role}:${issueId}` spawnKey into resolveModel/determineModel (visible in the diff at each call site).
determineModel returns a concrete `model` override unchanged — never resampling it — locking the sample-once invariant.
On restart/resume the restart path passes the persisted state.json model as the override, so resolveModel returns it without resampling.
The harness for a sampled spawn is resolved from the sampled model (resolveHarness receives the concrete sampled model, so a work distribution can yield mixed harnesses across agents).
RolesPanel: accept and display a distribution (read-through, no editor yet)

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/RolesPanel.tsx add local types `WeightedModelRef { model: ModelRef; weight: number }` and `RoleModelRef = ModelRef | WeightedModelRef[]`; widen RoleConfig.model and RoleConfigPatch.model to RoleModelRef. Update getRoleModel to return RoleModelRef and saveRoleConfig to accept an array model. When a top-level role's model is an array, render a distribution SUMMARY chip (e.g. '70% kimi-k2.7-code / 30% gpt-5.5', percentages computed from relative weights) instead of feeding the array to the single-select ModelPicker (which must not receive an array). Single-model roles and all sub-role pickers render exactly as today. This bead is display/read-through only; the editor is the next bead.

items.title: A role whose GET settings model is an array renders a distribution summary chip showing each model and its computed percentage.
A single-model role renders unchanged (existing dropdown + 'Default:' chip).
npm run typecheck produces no new errors with getRoleModel returning and saveRoleConfig accepting WeightedModelRef[].
When a role model is an array, the single-select ModelPicker is not rendered with an array value (no runtime crash); the summary is shown instead.
RolesPanel: single↔distribution toggle and weighted-rows editor for top-level roles

narrative.Action: Extend RolesPanel.tsx (top-level roles only) with a 'Single model' ↔ 'Distribution' toggle. Distribution mode renders a list of rows, each = the existing ModelPicker (for the entry model) + a positive-integer weight input + a remove button, plus an 'Add model' button and a live computed percentage per row (weight / sum). Saving sends model as WeightedModelRef[] through the existing PUT /api/settings (saveRoleConfig). Toggling back to single sends a scalar ModelRef. Sub-role pickers remain single-model only (no toggle). Add helper text stating weights are relative and each spawn is distributed statistically. Follow pan-style-guide: reuse the existing select/input classes, muted text, no pill badges, no decorative color.

items.title: Toggling a top-level role to 'Distribution' shows editable weighted rows (model select + weight input + remove button).
'Add model' appends a row, 'remove' deletes a row, and editing a weight updates the displayed percentage live.
Saving in distribution mode persists model as a WeightedModelRef[] via PUT; toggling back to single persists a scalar ModelRef.
Each sub-role picker shows only the single-model dropdown and displays no distribution toggle.
The panel displays helper text explaining relative weights and per-spawn statistical distribution.
